proven.claim: At line 20, the while-loop invariant guarantees match !== null.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; At line 20, the while-loop invariant guarantees match !== null.
; Prove: match_is_null = 1 is unreachable when the loop body is executing.
; Method: precondition (loop entered) + transition (loop condition semantics)
;         + negation (match is null) → expect unsat.

(declare-const loop_body_entered Int)
(declare-const match_is_null Int)

; Transition: while body is entered only when exec() returned non-null
(assert (=> (= loop_body_entered 1) (= match_is_null 0)))

; At line 20, the loop body is executing
(assert (= loop_body_entered 1))

; Negation of property: match is null at this point
(assert (= match_is_null 1))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — match_is_null=1 contradicts match_is_null=0 derived from loop entry

; PRINCIPLE: P6
; "(check-sat)" has exactly 11 characters.
; content = match[1]!.trim(); for lazy [\s\S]*?, content may be "".
; Prove: content_length < 11 → includes("(check-sat)") = false → filter_passes = 0.
; Method: transitions + negation of property → expect unsat.

(declare-const content_length Int)
(declare-const token_length Int)
(declare-const filter_passes Int)

; Transition: the target substring has exactly 11 characters
(assert (= token_length 11))

; Transition: includes() cannot return true when content is shorter than the token
(assert (=> (< content_length token_length) (= filter_passes 0)))

; Precondition: non-negative length, shorter than token
(assert (>= content_length 0))
(assert (< content_length token_length))

; Negation: short content somehow passes the filter
(assert (= filter_passes 1))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — no string of length < 11 can contain an 11-character token

violations.claim: content.includes("(check-sat)") is a substring match — it fires on
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; content.includes("(check-sat)") is a substring match — it fires on
; comment lines containing the token ("; ... (check-sat) ...") as well as
; actual SMT commands. A block where (check-sat) is only in a comment passes
; line 20 but contains no executable check-sat command.
;
; Model: total occurrences = comment occurrences + command occurrences.
; Code checks total >= 1; it should check command_occurrences >= 1.

(declare-const occurrences_in_comments Int)
(declare-const occurrences_as_commands Int)
(declare-const total_occurrences Int)

; Transition: includes() observes the total across all positions in the string
(assert (= total_occurrences (+ occurrences_in_comments occurrences_as_commands)))

; Transition: includes() returns true when total >= 1 — the filter passes
(assert (>= total_occurrences 1))

; Domain constraints: counts are non-negative
(assert (>= occurrences_in_comments 0))
(assert (>= occurrences_as_commands 0))

; Violation: block included but (check-sat) appears only inside comment lines
(assert (>= occurrences_in_comments 1))
(assert (= occurrences_as_commands 0))

(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — a block with (check-sat) only in comments passes line-20 filter
; Witness: occurrences_in_comments=1, occurrences_as_commands=0, total_occurrences=1

violations.claim: The filter at line 20 passes blocks with any number of (check-sat) occurrences.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; The filter at line 20 passes blocks with any number of (check-sat) occurrences.
; verifyBlock expects exactly one Z3 result token after trimming.
; A block with N > 1 check-sat commands produces N output lines — verifyBlock errors.
;
; Model the chain: check_sat_count → z3_output_lines → result_matches_expected.

(declare-const check_sat_count Int)
(declare-const z3_output_lines Int)
(declare-const result_matches_expected Int)

; Transition: each (check-sat) in the block causes Z3 to emit one answer line
(assert (= z3_output_lines check_sat_count))

; Transition: verifyBlock compares trimmed output to three single-word constants;
; multi-line output never equals a single word
(assert (=> (> z3_output_lines 1) (= result_matches_expected 0)))

; Code model: line-20 filter only requires count >= 1
(assert (>= check_sat_count 1))

; Violation: multiple (check-sat) pass line 20 but produce an error in verifyBlock
(assert (> check_sat_count 1))
(assert (= result_matches_expected 0))

(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — blocks with 2+ (check-sat) pass line 20 but error in verifyBlock
; Witness: check_sat_count=2, z3_output_lines=2, result_matches_expected=0
